expectedBehavior: Dispatches 6 parallel verification agents covering distinct quality dimensions
Launches visual capture agent in parallel with Phase 2 agents
Produces 0-10 scores across code quality, security, coverage, API, UI, and visual
Calculates weighted composite score using 8 dimensions and letter grade (A-F)
Generates gallery.html with base64-embedded screenshots and AI evaluations
Generates improvement suggestions with effort/impact prioritization

testCases.query: Just run the tests for the user profile feature
expectedBehavior: Runs backend and frontend test suites in parallel
Reports coverage data and pass/fail counts
Skips security audit and UI/UX analysis phases
Skips visual capture phase entirely
Focuses on test-generator agent only for targeted test execution

testCases.query: Run a security audit on the payment module
expectedBehavior: Focuses on security-auditor agent for dedicated vulnerability scanning
Checks OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities against the codebase patterns
Validates secrets and credential handling follows secure storage practices
Applies blocking rules from policy-as-code if configured

testCases.query: Quick check if my changes are ready to merge
expectedBehavior: Runs tests only without detailed analysis
Skips nuanced grading and improvement suggestions
Provides simple pass/fail verdict without detailed scoring breakdown
Completes quickly compared to full verification

testCases.query: How does the verify skill collect evidence for scoring code quality?
expectedBehavior: Runs git diff and git log commands in parallel in one message for context gathering
Executes backend and frontend test suites in parallel with coverage reporting enabled
Uses --cov=app and --cov-report=json for machine-readable coverage data collection
Captures screenshots via agent-browser and saves to verification-output folder
Gathers evidence from multiple sources before producing any quality scores or grades

testCases.query: What scoring scale does the verify skill use and what grades mean merge-ready?
expectedBehavior: Uses 0 to 10 scale with decimals for nuanced scoring rather than binary pass or fail
Grades A (9-10) and B (7-8.9) are READY FOR MERGE while D and F are BLOCKED
Calculates composite score as weighted sum across 8 quality dimensions including visual
Prioritizes improvements by impact divided by effort ratio to focus on high-value fixes

testCases.query: Verify the dashboard feature with visual evidence
expectedBehavior: Auto-detects project framework from package.json signals
Starts dev server using framework-appropriate command
Discovers routes via framework-aware scanning (App Router, Pages, React Router, etc.)
Uses agent-browser to navigate and screenshot each route (max 20)
Evaluates each screenshot with Claude vision for layout, a11y, and content issues
Generates self-contained gallery.html with base64-embedded screenshots
Includes visual score (0-10) in composite grading calculation
Detects no frontend framework when project is API-only
Skips visual capture phase with informational notice
Redistributes visual dimension weight proportionally to other 7 dimensions
Proceeds with full verification using remaining 6 agents without blocking

testCases.query: Verify the signup flow with visual feedback
expectedBehavior: Completes standard verification including visual capture
Detects agentation MCP availability via ToolSearch
Offers user choice to annotate live UI before finalizing
If accepted: watches for annotations, dispatches ui-feedback agent for fixes
Re-screenshots affected routes and shows before/after diffs in gallery
Limits annotation-fix cycles to max 3 rounds by default
